1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: the FULL-CORPUS gate for the slice must pass, not a
43	   fixture subset (see "Full-corpus gates" below). A narrow fixture suite that
44	   passes while the corpus gate fails is not acceptance.
45	4. Integration: the open side may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
46	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
47	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
48	   with them (history retains them under GPL). Every integrator that touches a
49	   slice with a full-corpus gate MUST run that gate before declaring green.
50	
51	## Full-corpus gates (standard, not optional)
52	
53	Each slice has a fast oracle gate over the whole 419-file example corpus,
54	diffing the slice's output against the Haskell reference cache. These are the
55	sealed implementer's primary data source (run between build and the HS cache
56	every iteration) AND the integrator's mandatory acceptance check. Both take
57	`RS_PATH` (point at any build) and `ALLOWLIST` (subset while iterating).
58	
59	- Wellformedness: `scripts/wf_gate.sh` — runs the binary WITHOUT `--prove`
60	  (the wf block prints at theory-load, ~1s/file; full corpus ~72s at JOBS=6),
61	  extracts the `/* WARNING … */` block, diffs vs `scripts/.hs_file_cache`.
62	- Web: `scripts/web_parity.sh` — crawls HS and RS interactive servers per
63	  theory, semantic-diffs the response manifests vs `scripts/.web_hs_cache`.
64	- Full batch (proof output, slow ~30-60min): `scripts/corpus_file_diff.sh` —
65	  the ground-truth `--prove` byte diff; run at milestones, not every iteration.
66	
67	Once a slice's ported code is DELETED, the integrated module IS the sealed
68	reimplementation, so running the gate against the tree observes the sealed
69	code's own output vs the reference — legitimate feedback, not observation of
70	replaced code. For a not-yet-integrated slice the tree still holds the ported
71	code, so gate only the workspace build (via `RS_PATH`), never the stock tree.
72

1	# Clean-room task: theory PRETTY-PRINTER
2	
3	Build a standalone Rust crate `pretty-clean` in `workspace/` that reimplements,
4	from BLACK-BOX BEHAVIOR ONLY, the tamarin-prover binary's **theory echo** — the
5	`theory <name> begin … end` text it prints at theory-load. Given a
6	parsed/closed theory (data model in `interface/ast_types.rs`), produce
7	byte-identical render text and the API in `interface/required_api.md`.
8	
9	## The observable boundary
10	
11	`oracle/pretty_oracle.sh <file.spthy>` runs the Haskell binary **WITHOUT
12	`--prove`** and prints the EXTRACTED THEORY ECHO — exactly the bytes you must
13	reproduce. Running without `--prove` is deliberate and load-bearing:
14	
15	* the echo prints at theory-load (~0.25 s/file), so the gate is fast; and
16	* **no proof search runs**, so every lemma renders `by sorry` and NO proof
17	  tree is emitted. The proof/constraint-system renderer is solver output, not
18	  pretty-printer surface, and is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE (see "Stays ported").
19	
20	The extracted block is `theory <name> begin … end` MINUS two trailing
21	formal-comment blocks the tool appends inside that span and everything after
22	`end`:
23	
24	* the wellformedness report (`/* All wellformedness checks were successful. */`
25	  or the multi-line `/*\nWARNING …\n*/`) — a **separate slice** owned by the
26	  wellformedness cluster; do not reimplement it here;
27	* the volatile `/*\nGenerated from: …\n*/` build stamp (version / git rev /
28	  compile time); and
29	* the `====… summary of summaries …` after `end` (carries processing time).
30	
31	Interior comments — per-rule `variants (modulo AC)` blocks, `has exactly the
32	trivial AC variant`, and `guarded formula characterizing all counter-examples`
33	— are KEPT: they are your output. The extraction awk is embedded in the oracle
34	and in the acceptance gate identically, so
35	`diff <(pretty_oracle.sh f) <(pretty-clean-render f)` == the gate verdict.
36	
37	## Acceptance gate (full corpus, primary oracle AND integration check)
38	
39	`/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/scripts/pretty_gate.sh` runs the binary at `RS_PATH`
40	(point it at your build) no-prove over the whole 419-file corpus, extracts the
41	theory echo, and diffs it against a no-prove Haskell reference cache
42	(`scripts/.hs_pretty_cache`, auto-filled on first run). It reuses the batch
43	gate's `ckey`/`flags`/`strip_env` machinery so per-file canonical flags stay
44	identical. Output TSV: `relpath  MATCH|DIFF|SKIP_*  diffcount`. Warm-cache full
45	corpus ≈ 72 s at JOBS=6. Run it every iteration; `ALLOWLIST` subsets it while
46	you work a single sub-target. **Acceptance is the FULL-corpus gate green, not a
47	fixture subset** (the wf-regression lesson).
48	
49	Measured baseline of the CURRENT ported tree against this gate: **403 MATCH /
50	16 DIFF / 0 SKIP**. All 16 DIFFs are the single `features/auto-sources/spore/*`
51	family and are NOT pretty-printer divergences (see "Stays ported"). The
52	pure-render surface is at 100% today, so your target is: keep every one of the
53	403 green while the ported renderer is deleted underneath you.
54	
55	## Sub-target decomposition (each independently gate-checkable via ALLOWLIST)
56	
57	Ranked by (individual-author yield ÷ isolation) — a good sealed round takes ONE
58	cleanly-observable sub-component at a time. Corpus counts are over the 419-file
59	parity corpus.
60	
61	| # | sub-target | exercised by | ported LOC | isolation | authors it dents |
62	|---|------------|-------------|-----------|-----------|------------------|
63	| **R1** | **term core + signature block** — `Term`→text (operators `^ * ⊕ ++ %+`, pairs `<..>`, `exp`, `diff`, sorts `~ $ # %`, constants `'x'`, `.idx`) AND the `builtins:`/`functions:`/`equations:` block | builtins 319, functions 229, equations 137; `^`251 `*`(mult) `⊕`38 `++`/mset 89 nat 2 | term `crates/tamarin-term/src/pretty.rs` ~410; sig fns in `pretty_theory.rs` (`render_signature`/`render_fun_syms`/`render_equations`) ~130 | HIGHEST — term is a pure leaf (`Term`→`String`, no solver), the foundation every other sub-target reuses; signature is the first block, minimal wrapping | term.rs: meiersi, beschmi, jdreier, PhilipLukertWork, rsasse, charlie-j, BTom-GH, rkunnema |
64	| **R2** | **rule rendering** — facts + premises/actions/conclusions `[..] --[..]-> [..]`, rule attributes, `variants (modulo AC)` text (substitutions supplied), loop-breakers | rules 332 | `render_rule`/`render_rule_body`/`render_ac_variants_block`/`rule_attributes_doc` in `pretty_theory.rs` ~600 | MODERATE — reuses R1 term/fact; variant SUBSTITUTIONS come pre-computed from the solver, you render them | bulk of `pretty_theory.rs` (25-author header) |
65	| **R3** | **restriction / lemma FORMULA rendering** — `∀ ∃ ⇒ ∧ ∨ ¬ ⊤ ⊥ @ < = ⊏ last(..)`, quantifier binder allocation, temporal vars `#i`, the guarded-formula comment | lemmas 384, restrictions 179, exists-trace 296 | `pretty_formula.rs` ~2800 (formula + guarded); lemma/restriction wrappers in `pretty_theory.rs` ~300 | MODERATE — reuses R1 for atom term args; formula layout is its own file | `pretty_formula.rs`: meiersi, beschmi, jdreier, PhilipLukertWork, rkunnema, rsasse, Hong-Thai, BTom-GH, charlie-j, arcz |
66	| **R4** | **macros / predicates blocks** | macros 9, predicates 12 | `render_parsed_macros`/`render_predicate` ~120 | VERY HIGH — tiny, self-contained | mop-up; no unique authors |
67	| — | **proof / heuristic / constraint-system annotations** | (only under `--prove` / web) | `pretty_system.rs` 702, proof fns in `pretty_theory.rs`, `pretty_hpj` proof paths | — | **STAYS PORTED** (solver-entangled — see below) |
68	
69	Recommended order: **R1 → R2 → R3 → R4**. R1 unblocks R2 and R3 (both consume
70	term rendering). Do R1 first: it is the deepest, most-reused, most-isolated
71	core, and it is what makes `pretty_theory.rs` (the 25-author file carrying the
72	pseudonymous web authors) deletable once R2/R3 also land.
73	
74	## Layout engine (NOT part of the erasure surface)
75	
76	Every renderer builds a HughesPJ `Doc` and renders it at width 110 / ribbon 73.
77	That layout algebra is BSD-licensed (Haskell `pretty-1.1.3.6`) and has ALREADY
78	been clean-roomed by the graphdot cluster
79	(`../graphdot/workspace/graph-clean/src/pretty.rs`, from
80	`../graphdot/sanctioned/pretty-1.1.3.6`). REUSE it — do not re-derive the
81	fitting logic from the GPL side. Your job is the tamarin-specific part: WHICH
82	combinators, in what nesting, with what literal strings and glyphs.
83	
84	## Solver-entangled inputs — pure render vs. "stays ported"
85	
86	The renderer is a `Theory → text` function ONLY for the surface below. Some
87	displayed data is COMPUTED by the ported closure/solver; the clean crate
88	RENDERS it but is handed it pre-computed, and the computation stays ported.
89	
90	CLEANLY REIMPLEMENTABLE (pure render — R1–R4):
91	* term rendering; signature block; facts; rules; macros/predicates;
92	  formula & restriction & lemma-statement rendering; the guarded-formula
93	  comment TEXT (given the guarded formula); the `variants (modulo AC)` block
94	  TEXT (given the substitutions).
95	
96	STAYS PORTED (solver/closure output, NOT pure `Theory→text`):
97	* **Proof trees / proof methods / constraint-system pane** (`pretty_system.rs`,
98	  the `pp_proof`/`solve_goal_to_doc`/`pp_contradiction` paths in
99	  `pretty_theory.rs`). Only rendered under `--prove` or in the web UI, so they
100	  are OUTSIDE the no-prove echo the gate observes. Leave them ported.
101	* **The DATA feeding R2/R3's comments**: the AC-variant substitutions come from
102	  Maude (`tools/rule_variants`), the guarded-formula negation from the guarded
103	  transform, the closed signature (merged/sorted symbols) from theory closure.
104	  The clean crate consumes these; the transforms stay ported.
105	* **`--auto-sources` injection** (the 16 gate DIFFs): with `--auto-sources` the
106	  closer injects `AUTO_IN_TERM_*`/`AUTO_OUT_TERM_*` action facts and an
107	  `AUTO_typing [sources]` lemma into the CLOSED theory. The RS no-prove path
108	  does not perform this injection, so its echo lacks them vs the HS reference —
109	  a **closure/preprocessing** gap, upstream of the renderer, not a pretty-print
110	  bug. Exclude this family via ALLOWLIST while iterating (see
111	  `round1/exclude_auto_sources.txt`); it is not the pretty slice's to fix.
112	
113	## Method requirements (per PROTOCOL.md)
114	
115	* Derive every glyph, operator precedence, wrap column, spacing and ordering by
116	  experimenting against `oracle/pretty_oracle.sh`; take exact output strings
117	  from observed oracle output (compatibility content, never memory).
118	* Log every oracle query's purpose in `workspace/QUERIES.log` (one line each).
119	* Maintain `workspace/BEHAVIOR.md`: the growing behavioral spec you infer
120	  (operator → glyph, precedence/parenthesization, wrap width, section order).
121	  A deliverable equal in weight to the code.
122	* Tests: per sub-target, a fixture (AST value as Rust constructors) + expected
123	  render snippet, asserted against your impl AND spot-checked against the
124	  oracle. Integration truth is the full-corpus gate.
125	* Dependencies: std only (plus the reused BSD Doc engine). You may NOT parse
126	  `.spthy` yourself; your input is the `ast_types.rs` model.

4	`theory <name> begin … end` text rendered at theory-load. This is the residual

1	# Required public API (interoperability surface) — pretty-clean
2	
3	The clean crate must expose these entry points so the open side can wire it in
4	behind thin adapters that translate the live `tamarin_parser::ast` /
5	closed-theory values into the crate's own AST subset. Every function returns
6	text that is byte-identical to the oracle's no-prove theory echo for the
7	corresponding fragment. Names are indicative; the boundary that matters is
8	"one entry point per observable sub-target, each independently gate-checkable".
9	
10	```rust
11	// ── (top) whole theory echo: `theory <name> begin … end` (minus the wf
12	//    comment and the Generated-from stamp, which are appended by callers). ──
13	pub fn render_theory(thy: &Theory, sig: &Signature) -> String;
14	
15	// ── (a) signature block: builtins / functions / equations declarations ──
16	pub fn render_signature_block(sig: &Signature) -> String;
17	
18	// ── (b) macros / predicates blocks ──
19	pub fn render_macros(macros: &[Macro]) -> String;        // "macros: …"
20	pub fn render_predicates(preds: &[Predicate]) -> String; // "predicates: …"
21	
22	// ── (c) rule rendering: one multiset-rewrite rule, plus fact & TERM render ──
23	pub fn render_rule(rule: &Rule, variants: Option<&AcVariants>) -> String;
24	pub fn render_fact(fact: &Fact) -> String;               // `Foo( a, b )`
25	pub fn render_term(term: &Term) -> String;               // THE deep core
26	
27	// ── (d) restriction / lemma FORMULA rendering ──
28	pub fn render_restriction(r: &Restriction) -> String;
29	pub fn render_lemma(l: &Lemma, guarded: Option<&Guarded>) -> String;
30	pub fn render_formula(f: &Formula) -> String;            // ∀ ∃ ⇒ ∧ ∨ ¬ @ ⊏ …
31	
32	// Layout primitive (BSD-provenance, NOT part of the GPL-erasure surface):
33	// the HughesPJ `Doc` engine + combinators. Reuse the graphdot cluster's
34	// clean-room engine (workspace/graph-clean/src/pretty.rs, derived from
35	// sanctioned/pretty-1.1.3.6). Width = 110, ribbon = 73 for the theory echo.
36	```
37	
38	`Theory`, `Term`, `Fact`, `Formula`, `Atom`, `Rule`, `Lemma`, `Restriction`,
39	`Macro`, `Predicate`, `VarSpec`, `SortHint`, `BinOp`: see `ast_types.rs`.
40	
41	`Signature`, `AcVariants`, `Guarded` are pre-computed inputs supplied by the
42	ported closure/solver (SPEC.md "Solver-entangled inputs"); the clean crate
43	only RENDERS them, and their observable text is learned from the oracle.

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Black-box oracle for the THEORY PRETTY-PRINTER clean-room.
3	#
4	# Runs the sanctioned Haskell tamarin-prover on a .spthy file WITHOUT --prove
5	# (the theory echo prints at theory-load; no proof search) and, by default,
6	# prints the EXTRACTED THEORY-ECHO BLOCK — the exact `theory <name> begin …
7	# end` text your reimplementation must reproduce byte-for-byte.
8	#
9	#   pretty_oracle.sh <file.spthy> [extra args]   # -> extracted theory echo
10	#   RAW=1 pretty_oracle.sh <file.spthy> [args]   # -> full HS output (2>&1),
11	#                                                   #    for parse errors / the
12	#                                                   #    wf block you are NOT
13	#                                                   #    reimplementing.
14	#
15	# The extracted block DROPS the trailing wellformedness comment (a separate
16	# slice) and the volatile `/* Generated from: … */` build stamp, and everything
17	# after `end` (the summary-of-summaries).  Interior comments — rule AC-variant
18	# blocks and `guarded formula characterizing …` — are KEPT; they are your
19	# output.  This is identical to the extraction the acceptance gate applies to
20	# both sides, so `diff <(pretty_oracle.sh f) <(your_binary f | extract)` == the
21	# gate's verdict.
22	echo 1000 > /proc/self/oom_score_adj 2>/dev/null || true
23	ulimit -v 25165824 2>/dev/null || true
24	export PATH="/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin:$PATH"
25	HS="${HS_PATH:-/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover-testing/.stack-work/install/x86_64-linux-tinfo6/ec0cb11b1bfcf8776d45e0357bbc6d6ff2077f9222735af22115429c8cdfcef1/9.6.7/bin/tamarin-prover}"
26	f="$1"; shift
27	DERIVCHECK_TIMEOUT="${DERIVCHECK_TIMEOUT:-10}"
28	
29	if [ -n "${RAW:-}" ]; then
30	    exec timeout "${ORACLE_TIMEOUT:-60}" "$HS" --derivcheck-timeout="$DERIVCHECK_TIMEOUT" "$f" "$@" 2>&1
31	fi
32	
33	timeout "${ORACLE_TIMEOUT:-60}" "$HS" --derivcheck-timeout="$DERIVCHECK_TIMEOUT" "$f" "$@" 2>/dev/null \
34	| grep -v -e '^Git revision:' -e '^Compiled at:' -e '^[[:space:]]*processing time:' -e '^[[:space:]]*analyzed:' \
35	| awk '
36	    /^theory /              { cap=1 }
37	    !cap                    { next }
38	    /^\/\* All wellformedness checks were successful\. \*\/$/ { next }
39	    /^\/\*$/ {
40	        if ((getline nxt) > 0) {

43	                next
44	            }

48	    }

1	//! pretty-clean: a clean-room reimplementation of the tamarin-prover theory
2	//! pretty-printer (the `theory <name> begin … end` echo), derived purely from
3	//! black-box oracle behavior. See ../../SPEC.md for the observable boundary and
4	//! the R1–R4 sub-target decomposition, and workspace/BEHAVIOR.md for the
5	//! inferred behavioral spec you build up as you probe the oracle.
6	//!
7	//! SCAFFOLD ONLY. Every render entry point below is an UNIMPLEMENTED stub so
8	//! the crate compiles and the test harness runs (and fails meaningfully) from
9	//! day one. Fill in bodies one sub-target at a time, gating each against
10	//! `scripts/pretty_gate.sh` with an ALLOWLIST (see round1/).
11	//!
12	//! Recommended order: R1 (term + signature) → R2 (rules) → R3 (formula /
13	//! lemma / restriction) → R4 (macros / predicates).
14	
15	pub mod ast;
16	pub mod doc; // BSD HughesPJ Doc engine — reuse graphdot's clean-room port.
17	
18	pub mod formula; // R3
19	pub mod lemma; // R3
20	pub mod macros; // R4
21	pub mod rule; // R2
22	pub mod signature; // R1
23	pub mod term; // R1 (deep core)
24	pub mod theory; // top-level assembly
25	
26	pub use ast::*;
27	
28	/// Whole theory echo: `theory <name> begin … end`, minus the wf comment and
29	/// the Generated-from stamp (both appended by the caller / stripped by the
30	/// gate). Top-level assembly of the R1–R4 sub-targets. See `theory::render`.
31	pub fn render_theory(thy: &ast::Theory, sig: &ast::Signature) -> String {
32	    theory::render(thy, sig)
33	}

2	//!
3	//! This is the clean crate's OWN model — you refine it as the oracle teaches
4	//! you what each construct renders to. The integration-time surface (what the
5	//! open side adapts live values into) is `../../interface/ast_types.rs`; keep
6	//! these shapes compatible with it as you go, but drive the design from the

12	// ── R1: terms (the deep core) ───────────────────────────────────────────────

14	/// Multiset-rewriting term. Rendered by `term::render`.
15	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
16	pub enum Term {
17	    Var(VarSpec),
18	    PubLit(String),   // 'name'
19	    FreshLit(String), // ~'name'-style fresh constant
20	    NatLit(String),
21	    Number(u64),
22	    NumberOne,
23	    NatOne,
24	    DhNeutral,
25	    App(String, Vec<Term>),           // f(a, b, …)
26	    AlgApp(String, Box<Term>, Box<Term>), // exp(a,b) -> a^b, etc.
27	    Pair(Vec<Term>),                  // <a, b, c>
28	    Diff(Box<Term>, Box<Term>),       // diff(a, b)
29	    BinOp(BinOp, Box<Term>, Box<Term>),
30	    PatMatch(Box<Term>),
31	}
32	
33	/// Associative-commutative / infix operators and their surface glyphs — learn
34	/// the exact glyph and precedence/parenthesization from the oracle.
35	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
36	pub enum BinOp {
37	    Exp,     // ^
38	    Mult,    // *
39	    Union,   // ++
40	    Xor,     // ⊕  (U+2295)
41	    NatPlus, // %+
42	}
43	
44	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
45	pub struct VarSpec {
46	    pub name: String,

51

53	pub enum SortHint {
54	    Msg,
55	    Pub,    // $x
56	    Fresh,  // ~x
57	    Node,   // #i
58	    Nat,    // %n

60	    Untagged,
61	}

115	    True,

119	    Or(Box<Formula>, Box<Formula>),
120	    Implies(Box<Formula>, Box<Formula>),
121	    Iff(Box<Formula>, Box<Formula>),

136	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]

16	The input files for the Tamarin prover have the extension .spthy, which is
17	short for 'security protocol theory'. A security protocol theory specifies
18	
19	  1. the signature and equational theory to use for the message algebra,
20	  2. the set of multiset rewriting rules modeling the protocol and
21	     the adversary capabilities, and
22	  3. the guarded trace properties whose satisfiability or validity we wish to
23	     check for this set of multiset rewriting rules.

25	We explain each of these parts where they occur in the following security
26	protocol theory. Before we start, let us add a few notes on the syntax.
27	As you probably noticed, comments are C-style. All identifiers are
28	case-sensitive. The parser is layout-insensitive, i.e., your are free to use
29	whitespace as it suits you. We provide a complete specification of the input
30	syntax in the REFERENCE MANUAL (available at doc/MANUAL.md).
31	
32	
33	Modeling a security protocol
34	----------------------------
35	
36	Every security protocol theory starts with a header of the following form.
37	*/

40	begin
41	
42	/*
43	Obviously, you can replace 'Tutorial' with any name you like to give
44	your theory. After 'begin', you can declare function symbols,
45	equations that they must satisfy, multiset rewriting rules, and lemmas
46	specifying security properties. Moreover, you can also insert formal
47	comments, to structure your theory. We give examples of each of these
48	elements while modeling a simple protocol.
49	
50	In this protocol, a client C generates a fresh symmetric key 'k', encrypts it
51	with the public key of a server 'S' and sends it to 'S'. The server confirms
52	the receipt of the key by sending back its hash to the client. In
53	Alice-and-Bob notation the protocol would read as follows.
54	
55	  C -> S: aenc{k}pk(S)
56	  C <- S: h(k)
57

59	guarantees. We can prove that from the perspective of the client, the freshly

# BEHAVIOR.md — inferred behavioral spec of the theory echo (R1: term core + signature block)

Every claim below traces to a logged oracle probe (QUERIES.log) or to the
pre-materialized round-1 captures (`round1/targets/*.hs.txt`, themselves oracle
output). Notation: "target:<file>" = observed in that capture; "probe:<name>" =
observed via a constructed .spthy run through `oracle/pretty_oracle.sh`.

## Layout engine parameters

Rendering is HughesPJ-Doc-shaped. Width 110 / ribbon 73 (SPEC-provided; wrap
observations consistent: `builtins:` line of NumberSubtermTests wraps when the
next item would end past column 73).

## Signature section (initial observations from targets; to be pinned by probes)

Shape (target:cav13_DH_example, target:Tutorial, target:features_private_function_symbols_test):

```
// Function signature and definition of the equational theory E

builtins: <canonical-ordered list>          (line absent if none survive)
functions: <sorted list>
equations: <sorted list>
```

* Header comment always present, followed by ONE blank line.
* `builtins:` echoes a CANONICAL order, not source order:
  input `natural-numbers, multiset, diffie-hellman, xor, bilinear-pairing, hashing`
  → echo `diffie-hellman, bilinear-pairing, multiset, natural-numbers, xor`
  (target:features_multiset_NumberSubtermTests vs its source).
  `hashing` disappears from the line (expands into functions).
  `signing` disappears likewise; `bilinear-pairing` INDUCES `diffie-hellman`
  (target:sp14_Joux: source `bilinear-pairing, signing, multiset` → echo
  `diffie-hellman, bilinear-pairing, multiset` plus sign/verify/pk/true fns).
  `asymmetric-encryption` leaves NO builtins line (target:classic_NSLPK3).
* Base signature always contains `fst/1, pair/2, snd/1` and equations
  `fst(<x.1, x.2>) = x.1`, `snd(<x.1, x.2>) = x.2` (all targets).
* `functions:` items `name/arity`, sorted; attributes: `shk/0[private,constructor]`
  (target:cav13_DH_example — source wrote `shk/0 [private]`).
* `equations:` one-line when it fits; otherwise `equations:` alone then each
  equation on its own line at indent 4, trailing `,` on all but the last
  (target:Tutorial, target:classic_NSLPK3). NOT fill behavior: 3 short
  equations that could pack 2-per-line still go one-per-line → sep/vcat
  semantics, not fsep (target:Tutorial).
* `builtins:` wrap continuation aligns at column 10 (= after `builtins: `),
  fill behavior (4 items on line 1, `xor` alone on line 2)
  (target:features_multiset_NumberSubtermTests) → `text "builtins: " <> fsep`.

## Term rendering (initial observations from targets; to be pinned by probes)

* exp: `g^~x`, `x.10^inv(~x.7)`, `em(XB, XC)^~ekA` — infix `^`, no spaces.
* mult: `(~x.7*x.11)`, `(x.11*x.13*inv(x.14))` — flattened n-ary, `*`,
  no spaces, ALWAYS parenthesized (even as a function argument:
  `inv((~x.7*x.11))`, target:cav13_DH_example variants).
* xor: `(~x⊕~y)` — glyph U+2295, no spaces, parenthesized (target:features_xor_xor).
* multiset union: `($A++~x)`, `(a++a++a)` — `++`, flattened, parenthesized.
* nat plus: `(%x%+%1)`, `(%x%+%1%+%1%+%1)` — `%+`, flattened, parenthesized;
  nat-one constant `%1` (target:features_multiset_NumberSubtermTests).
* pair: `<a, b, c>` — right-nested pairs flatten, `, ` separators.
* App: `f(a, b)` — `, ` separated, NO space inside parens (contrast facts
  `Fr( ~tid )` which pad with spaces); nullary constants render bare (`shk`,
  `zero`, `true`, `f`).
* Sorts: `~x` fresh, `$A` pub, `#i` node (formulas), `%x` nat; untagged msg bare.
* Variable index: `.N` suffix when idx present: `x.1`, `~x.7`, `XB.10`;
  no suffix otherwise.
* Pub string constants: `'1'`, `'g'`, `'P'`.
* DH neutral: `DH_neutral` (target:cav13_DH_example variant 7).

# BEHAVIOR.md — inferred behavioral spec of the theory echo (R1: term core + signature block)

Every claim traces to a logged oracle probe (QUERIES.log) or a pre-materialized
round-1 capture (`round1/targets/*.hs.txt` — itself oracle output). Notation:
"target:<file>" = observed in that capture; "probe:<name>" = constructed
.spthy run through `oracle/pretty_oracle.sh` (files under
workspace/scratchpad/probes/, outputs kept beside them as `.out`).

## Layout engine

HughesPJ Doc at line width 110, ribbon 73 (SPEC-provided parameters; all wrap
observations below are reproduced by the sanctioned BSD engine at these
settings). Key engine consequences confirmed against probes:

* A one-line candidate is kept iff it fits within ribbon 73 measured from the
  current line position; an exactly-73-column line still fits
  (probe:e_mid — 73-column `equations:` one-liner kept; probe:b_all —
  builtins item that would end at column 75 wraps).
* Fill continuations align at the fill's origin column, and that indent does
  NOT consume ribbon: continuation content is measured from the alignment
  column (probe:b_all functions line 2 reaches display column 79 = 11 + 68;
  probe:t_wide App continuation reaches display column 78).
* Nested content (e.g. rule bodies at indent 3) likewise measures its ribbon
  from the nest, so lines up to 76 display columns appear at nest 3
  (probe:t_edge).

## Signature section

Overall shape (all targets/probes):

```
// Function signature and definition of the equational theory E
<blank>
builtins: …        (line ABSENT when no surviving entries)
functions: …       (always present — base symbols guarantee items)
equations: …       (always present — base equations)
```

The three declaration lines are adjacent (no blank lines between them).

### `builtins:` line

* Only these builtins keep a builtins-line entry: `diffie-hellman`,
  `bilinear-pairing`, `multiset`, `natural-numbers`, `xor`
  (probes:b_<name>). All others (hashing, signing, revealing-signing,
  symmetric-encryption, asymmetric-encryption, dest-*, locations-report)
  vanish from the line, contributing functions/equations instead
  (probes:b_*, b_locrep; target:classic_NSLPK3 has no builtins line).
* `bilinear-pairing` INDUCES `diffie-hellman` (probe:b_bilinear-pairing:
  echo `builtins: diffie-hellman, bilinear-pairing`; target:sp14_Joux).
* Canonical order regardless of source order: diffie-hellman <
  bilinear-pairing < multiset < natural-numbers < xor (probe:b_all — source
  order was scrambled; target:features_multiset_NumberSubtermTests).
* Duplicates collapse (probe:b_dupline).
* Layout: `text "builtins: " <> fsep (punctuate ',' names)` — items
  comma-separated, single space, fill-wrapped, continuation aligned at
  column 10 (after `builtins: `), comma attached to the preceding item
  (probe:b_all, target:features_multiset_NumberSubtermTests).

### `functions:` line

* Items render `name/arity` + attribute suffix, comma-separated.
* Attribute suffix (probe:f_attrs, probe:b_locrep, probes:b_dest-*):
  - public constructor → no suffix (`c/3`)
  - private constructor → `[private,constructor]`
  - public destructor → `[destructor]`
  - private destructor → `[private,destructor]`
  No space before `[`; no spaces inside.
* Sort: ASCII byte order on the name, case-sensitive (`Bb/1, Zz/1, a1/2, aa/1,
  cA/0` — probe:f_sort).
* Dedup: identical (name,arity) between user decls and builtin expansions, or
  repeated user decls, appear once (probe:f_dedup). Conflicting arity or
  attribute redeclarations are REJECTED upstream by the tool
  (probe:f_dedup2 raw error), so the renderer never sees them.
* Typed declarations (`kdf(bitstring):skey`) erase to `kdf/1`
  (probe:f_typed).
* Base symbols always present: `fst/1, pair/2, snd/1` (probe:b_none);
  with `dest-pairing` instead: `fst/1[destructor], pair/2, snd/1[destructor]`
  (probe:b_dest-pairing).
* Per-builtin function expansions (compatibility content, from probes
  b_hashing / b_asymmetric-encryption / b_signing / b_symmetric-encryption /
  b_revealing-signing / b_locrep / b_dest-*):
  - hashing → `h/1`
  - asymmetric-encryption → `adec/2, aenc/2, pk/1`
    (dest-asymmetric-encryption: `adec/2[destructor]`)
  - signing → `pk/1, sign/2, true/0, verify/3`
    (dest-signing: `verify/3[destructor]`)
  - symmetric-encryption → `sdec/2, senc/2`
    (dest-symmetric-encryption: `sdec/2[destructor]`)
  - revealing-signing → `getMessage/1, pk/1, revealSign/2, revealVerify/3,
    true/0`
  - locations-report → `check_rep/2[destructor], get_rep/1[destructor],
    rep/2[private,constructor], report/1`
  - diffie-hellman / bilinear-pairing / multiset / natural-numbers / xor →
    none (their operators/constants — one, inv, DH_neutral, zero, ++, %+ —
    never appear in `functions:`; probes b_diffie-hellman, b_xor, …).
* Layout: `text "functions: " <> fsep (punctuate ',' items)` — fill wrap,
  continuation aligned at column 11 (probe:b_revealing-signing, probe:b_all,
  probe:e_long).

### `equations:` block

* Header `equations:`, or `equations [convergent]:` when the user block is
  declared `[convergent]` (probe:e_conv).
* Base equations always present: `fst(<x.1, x.2>) = x.1`,
  `snd(<x.1, x.2>) = x.2` (probe:b_none).
* Per-builtin equation expansions (probes as above):
  - asymmetric-encryption → `adec(aenc(x.1, pk(x.2)), x.2) = x.1`
  - signing → `verify(sign(x.1, x.2), x.1, pk(x.2)) = true`
  - symmetric-encryption → `sdec(senc(x.1, x.2), x.2) = x.1`
  - revealing-signing → `getMessage(revealSign(x.1, x.2)) = x.1`,
    `revealVerify(revealSign(x.1, x.2), x.1, pk(x.2)) = true`
  - locations-report → `check_rep(rep(x.1, x.2), x.2) = x.1`,
    `get_rep(rep(x.1, x.2)) = x.1`
  - hashing → none
* Sort: byte order on the RENDERED equation string; discriminated by
  same-head pair `fst(<a, b>) = a` < `fst(<x.1, x.2>) = x.1`
  (probe:e_adedup) and by every multi-source list (probe:b_all).
* Dedup: EXACT duplicates collapse (probe:e_dup); alpha-equivalent but
  differently-named equations do NOT (probe:e_adedup keeps user
  `fst(<a, b>) = a` alongside the builtin fst equation).
* Layout: `sep (header : map (nest 4) (punctuate ',' eqDocs))` — i.e.
  all-or-nothing, NOT fill:
  - fits on one line (≤ 73) → `equations: e1, e2, e3` (probe:e_mid at
    exactly 73);
  - otherwise header alone, then EVERY equation on its own line at indent 4
    with trailing comma on all but the last — even when several would fit
    joined at indent 4 (probe:e_conv: joined length 71 still one-per-line).
* Each equation is `sep [lhsDoc, nest (-2) (text "= " <> rhsDoc)]`:
  one line `lhs = rhs`; when too long, `= rhs` drops to (equation indent − 2)
  (probe:e_long — `  = xlongvariablename1` at column 2 under equations at
  indent 4).

## Term rendering

Observed atoms:

| AST shape | rendered | provenance |
|---|---|---|
| var, untagged/msg | `x` | everywhere |
| var, fresh | `~x` | target:cav13 (`x:fresh` source → `~x`) |
| var, pub | `$A` | target:cav13 |
| var, node | `#i` | targets (lemma text) |
| var, nat | `%x` | target:NumberSubtermTests |
| var with index k>0 | `name.k` after the sigil (`~x.7`, `x.10`, `XB.10`) | targets: variants + builtin eqs |
| pub literal | `'g'`, `'hello_world'` | probe:t_pair |
| fresh literal | `~'n'` | probe:t_frlit |
| nat literal 1 (`%1`, `1:nat`) | `%1` | probe:t_nat |
| nat literal n (`2:nat`) | `%2` | probe:t_num2 |
| DH one (`1` in DH theory) | `one` | probe:t_one, probe:t_gone |
| DH neutral | `DH_neutral` | probe:t_gone, target:cav13 variant 7 |
| xor zero | `zero` (nullary-app form) | probe:t_xor |

* Application: `f(a1, a2)` — `text f <> "(" <> fsep (punctuate ',' args) <>

  aligns after the `(`, closing `)` attached to the last arg
  (probe:t_wide W2 — continuation line `ylongvariablename2,
  zlongvariablename3)`; probe:e_long). Nullary symbols render bare (`shk`,

* Pair: `<a, b, c>` — `"<" <> fcat (punctuate ", " elems) <> ">"`; elements
  carry an attached `", "` (a wrapped line ends with a trailing space —
  probe:t_wide W1 byte-checked), fill wrap aligns after `<`, `>` attached to
  the last element. Right-nested pairs flatten (`<x, <y, z>>` → `<x, y, z>`);
  a pair in NON-last position stays delimited: `<<x, y>, z>` (probe:t_pair).
  Source `pair(x, y)` arrives already normalized to a pair (probe:t_pair).

  trees: `('g'^~x)^~y` AND `'g'^(~x^~y)` both echo `'g'^~x^~y`
  (probe:t_exp2). No parentheses are ever added around exp or its operands
  (operand classes that need delimiting — AC ops — are self-parenthesizing).
* AC operators mult `*`, xor `⊕` (U+2295), multiset `++`, nat-plus `%+`:
  always wrapped in parens, no spaces, arguments flattened across BOTH sides
  regardless of source nesting: `(x⊕y⊕z)`, `(a++b++c)`, `(%x%+%y%+%z)`,

  the AC rule variants normalize (g^x)^y to `'g'^(~x*~y)`).
  The parens are intrinsic: they appear in every context (function argument
  `inv((~x.7*x.11))` — target:cav13 variants; fact argument `Out( (~x⊕~y) )`).
